notes.anchor: as Theophrastus would have it, a modest metaphor
notes.note: Theophrastus, Aristotle's successor as head of the Peripatos, wrote on style; Cicero invokes his doctrine that a metaphor should be modest and apt. The whole letter is a playful lesson on usage, treating Tiro as the standard (kanon) of Cicero's Latin.

notes.anchor: Thank you for the trouble you have taken over Cuspius
notes.note: Publius Cuspius, an equestrian friend of Cicero with business in the African tax-farming companies, on whose behalf Cicero repeatedly wrote letters of recommendation.
